source_checks.label_discipline: Panel bodies are axis lists (comparison-matrix form per the side-by-side panels pattern), not flow-node labels; panel titles are <=4 words. No bare unexplained acronyms — 'EO 14110' is paired with 'a US Presidential order' per corpus; 'NIST' paired with 'a US agency'.
source_checks.concept_fidelity: All content traces to corpus section 5.7 comparison table — three frameworks (EU AI Act / NIST AI RMF / EO 14110) and four axes (issuer, who-must-obey, reversibility, reach).
source_checks.scope_discipline: No scope-creep nodes. No Defense Production Act, no National Security Memorandum, no 5.12/5.13 content.

visual_checks.layout_note: Three panels rendered side by side (LR), each listing the same four axes in the same order — scannable left-to-right across frameworks. Matches the 'side-by-side comparison' intent.
visual_checks.color_note: EU AI Act = start/blue, NIST RMF = auto/purple, EO 14110 = hitl/yellow — three distinct tiers, all text legible.

findings.detail: Three panels stacked vertically instead of side-by-side as the intent requires.
Subgraph-title second line ('a binding law' etc.) clipped by the first node's top edge in every panel.

cycle_log.fix: Collapsed each framework into a single panel node containing its four axes as a bold-term + plain-meaning list; arranged the three panels side-by-side with invisible (~~~) links under flowchart LR. Headers moved into the panel body so nothing is clipped. Re-rendered and visually re-inspected: three legible side-by-side panels, no overlap, no truncation.

verdict: PASS — three side-by-side comparison panels (EU AI Act / NIST AI RMF / EO 14110) across four axes; layout matches intent, all labels legible, no scope-creep.
